---
title: "Creating A File Plan"
---

A file plan serves as a container of categories each linked to specific retention policies and allows you to manage content in different repositories, projects, or groups. It provides you with a systematic structure for storing records based on their type, classification, function, or department. This ensures that records are classified and retained, improving overall efficiency in locating and managing policies. Record administrators can create several file plans to represent different record categories' structures to support retention rules.

Note: Immediately after creating a Governance project, a new file plan, with the same name as the project, is automatically created and added to the list of file plans.
To create a file plan:

1.  Open your project in Studio Modeler.
1.  Go to All projects under the Projects section on the left pane.
2.  Select your project. The Governance project site is displayed.
2.  Create a File Plan:
- On the left pane, click the vertical ellipsis next to File Plan Configurations and select Create. ![](data:image/png;base64,) ![Create a File Plan from the File Plan Configurations menu]()
- On the upper part of the page, click Create, and then click Create File Plan. ![](data:image/png;base64,) ![Create a File Plan from the Create button]()
The Create File Plan window is displayed. ![Create File Plan window](data:image/png;base64,)
3.  Enter a name for your file plan in the File Plan Name field. For example, Onboarding Documents or Employment Records.  Note: You cannot enter a name that is already used by another file plan in the selected project. It is possible to have the same file plan name in two different projects.
4.  Click Create. The Governance project site containing File Plan Categories is displayed.
5.  To switch a file plan, expand three dots in the right upper corner and click Switch file plan.   ![A three dots button expanded displaying a 'Switch file plan' button](data:image/png;base64,)   The File plan configuration site in Studio Modeler is displayed.
6.  Select a file plan you want to access. You can find it on the left pane under the  Governance  section.
7.  Click Configure file plan to access a file plan. The File plan configuration site in Studio Modeler is displayed.
